summary: This is a brief announcement post about Cathy O'Neil's upcoming speaking engagements. She mentions modifying her 'Start Your Own Netflix' talk for Worcester Polytechnic Institute, planning an MSRI-Evans talk at Berkeley where she's deciding between her Netflix talk or a 'World Is Going To Hell' talk about algorithmic dangers, and hoping to visit the Noetherian Ring women in math group she participated in as an undergrad in the early 1990s.
key_points: She has multiple versions of talks: a technical Netflix recommendation system talk and a 'doomsday' talk about algorithmic harms
She's actively soliciting audience input on which talk to give at Berkeley
She fondly recalls the Noetherian Ring as an early supportive environment for women in math that helped her give her first talks
money_quotes: I still haven't decided whether to give a 'The World Is Going To Hell' talk, which would be kind of the technical version of my book
It really was a great example of how to create a supportive environment for collaborative math
key_insight: This administrative post reveals O'Neil's dual identity as both a technical mathematician interested in recommendation systems and a critic warning about algorithmic dangers, while showing her commitment to supporting women in math.
